thalliki_vandanam.name: Thalliki Vandanam
thalliki_vandanam.benefit: ₹15,000 per year per child studying in school

online: Click 'Apply Online' and login with Aadhaar OTP
Fill child details: name, class, school name, Aadhaar
Fill mother details: name, Aadhaar, bank account
Submit application and note the acknowledgment number
offline: Visit nearest MeeSeva center or Sachivalayam ward office
Fill the form with child and mother details
Attach photocopies of all required documents

thalliki_vandanam.disbursement: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to mother's bank account
thalliki_vandanam.deadline: Academic year cycle; apply before mid-year disbursement
thalliki_vandanam.description: Thalliki Vandanam is Andhra Pradesh's flagship school education assistance scheme under the TDP government's Super Six promises. It provides ₹15,000 per year per child to the mother to prevent school dropouts. The amount is split as ₹13,000 to the mother via DBT and ₹2,000 to the school for infrastructure. Eligibility requires the family to be below the income limit, hold a rice card, and the child must have 75% attendance in the previous academic year.
notes: Application must be submitted every academic year
Bank account must be in mother's name and NPCI-linked
Attendance certificate must be signed by school headmaster
Amount is credited in installments, usually mid-year

annadata_sukhibhava.name: Annadata Sukhibhava

offline: Visit nearest Rythu Bharosa Kendra or MeeSeva center

annadata_sukhibhava.processing_time: Disbursed in 3 installments per financial year
annadata_sukhibhava.disbursement: DBT to farmer's bank account (₹14,000 state + ₹6,000 PM-KISAN)
annadata_sukhibhava.deadline: Ongoing; enroll anytime during the financial year
annadata_sukhibhava.description: Annadata Sukhibhava is Andhra Pradesh's farmer income support scheme, replacing the previous YSR Rythu Bharosa. Combined with the central PM-KISAN scheme, eligible farmer families receive ₹20,000 per year via Direct Benefit Transfer. The scheme requires valid digitized land records, Aadhaar linkage, completed eKYC, and an active NPCI-mapped bank account. Income tax payers are excluded.
notes: Must have cultivable land with valid digitized records
eKYC is mandatory and must be renewed periodically
Bank account must be active and NPCI-mapped
PM-KISAN enrollment is automatically linked if already registered

ntr_bharosa_pension.name: NTR Bharosa Pension
ntr_bharosa_pension.benefit: ₹4,000 to ₹15,000 per month depending on category
documents: Age proof (for senior citizens: Aadhaar / Birth certificate / SSC marksheet)

online: Upload required documents based on category
offline: Visit nearest Sachivalayam ward office or MeeSeva center
- Senior: Age proof + Aadhaar + Ration card
- Widow: Death certificate + Aadhaar + Ration card
- Disabled: Disability certificate + Aadhaar + Ration card
Verification by ward volunteer within 15 days

ntr_bharosa_pension.processing_time: 30-45 days from application to first pension credit
ntr_bharosa_pension.disbursement: Monthly DBT to beneficiary's bank account (1st of every month)
ntr_bharosa_pension.description: NTR Bharosa Pension (previously YSR Pension Kanuka) is Andhra Pradesh's monthly social security pension scheme. It provides financial assistance to senior citizens aged 65+, widows, persons with disabilities, and workers in traditional occupations like weavers, toddy tappers, fishermen, transgender persons, and traditional cobblers. The pension amount ranges from ₹4,000 to ₹15,000 per month based on the category and severity of disability.
notes: Pension is credited on the 1st of every month
Beneficiary must be present for biometric verification periodically
If pension stops, visit ward secretariat immediately
Bedridden beneficiaries get higher amount with home verification
CKDU (Chronic Kidney Disease of Unknown etiology) patients get ₹10,000/month

deepam_2.name: Deepam 2.0
deepam_2.benefit: Free LPG gas cylinders (cylinder cost refunded within 48 hours)

benefit_breakdown.cylinder_cost: Full refund within 48 hours of purchase
benefit_breakdown.frequency: Multiple refills per year as per eligibility

documents: BPL certificate (if not on ration card)

online: Refund credited within 48 hours to bank account

fees.cylinder_purchase: Pay at dealer, get refunded in 48 hours

deepam_2.processing_time: Enrollment: 7-15 days; Refund: within 48 hours of each purchase
deepam_2.disbursement: Refund credited to linked bank account within 48 hours
deepam_2.description: Deepam 2.0 is one of the TDP government's Super Six schemes providing free LPG cylinders to eligible BPL families in Andhra Pradesh. The woman head of the household purchases the cylinder from an authorized dealer and the full cost is refunded to her bank account within 48 hours. The scheme promotes clean cooking fuel and reduces health hazards from traditional chulhas.
notes: LPG connection must be in the woman's name or jointly
Refund only for cylinders purchased from authorized dealers
Keep all purchase receipts until refund is credited
If refund delays beyond 48 hours, contact ward secretariat
Scheme is part of the government's clean energy initiative

pm_kisan.name: PM-KISAN
pm_kisan.benefit: ₹6,000 per year in three installments of ₹2,000 each
documents: Land ownership records (7/12, Adangal, or state equivalent)

online: Enter Aadhaar number and verify with OTP
Fill personal details: name, address, land details
Enter bank account details (must be NPCI-mapped)
Complete eKYC (biometric at CSC or OTP-based)
offline: Visit nearest Common Service Center (CSC) or agriculture office

fees.offline: Free (CSC may charge nominal service fee)

pm_kisan.disbursement: DBT of ₹2,000 every 4 months to registered bank account
pm_kisan.description: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi) is a central government scheme providing income support of ₹6,000 per year to farmer families across India. The amount is transferred in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each directly to the beneficiary's bank account through DBT. Eligible farmers must have cultivable land, valid land records, completed eKYC, and an NPCI-mapped bank account. Income tax payers, government employees, and professionals are excluded.
notes: eKYC is mandatory — complete at nearest CSC or online
Land records must be updated and digitized
Check beneficiary status at pmkisan.gov.in using Aadhaar
If installment is missed, contact agriculture department or CSC
Name in Aadhaar and bank account must match exactly

ayushman_bharat_pmjay.name: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY
ayushman_bharat_pmjay.name_telugu: ఆయుష్మాన్ భారత్ ప్రధాన మంత్రి జన్ ఆరోగ్య యోజన
ayushman_bharat_pmjay.benefit: ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary healthcare

online: Click 'Am I Eligible?' and enter mobile number
If eligible, complete e-KYC using Aadhaar OTP
Print or save digitally for hospital visits
offline: Visit nearest Ayushman Mitra center or Common Service Center
Or visit empanelled hospital — they can verify on the spot

ayushman_bharat_pmjay.disbursement: Cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals (no upfront payment)
ayushman_bharat_pmjay.description: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (PM-JAY) is the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme. It provides ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization. Eligibility is based on SECC 2011 deprivation criteria for rural households and occupational categories for urban households. Since 2024, all individuals aged 70 and above are universally covered regardless of income. Treatment is cashless at over 25,000 empanelled hospitals across India.
notes: Everyone aged 70+ is automatically eligible since 2024
Check eligibility first at beneficiary.nha.gov.in before applying
Carry PM-JAY e-card and Aadhaar to hospital
Treatment is completely cashless at empanelled hospitals
No need to pay and claim later — hospital bills settled directly by government
Over 1,500 medical procedures covered including surgery, cancer treatment, cardiac care

pmay_urban.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Urban 2.0)
pmay_urban.benefit: Home loan interest subsidy / housing assistance by income band

benefit_breakdown.EWS: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh (income up to ₹3 lakh/year)
benefit_breakdown.LIG: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.67 lakh (income ₹3-6 lakh/year)
benefit_breakdown.MIG_I: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.35 lakh (income ₹6-12 lakh/year)
benefit_breakdown.MIG_II: Interest subsidy up to ₹2.30 lakh (income ₹12-18 lakh/year)

documents: Income proof (salary slip / ITR / employer certificate / self-declaration)

online: Enter Aadhaar and verify with OTP
Upload income proof and property documents
Approach bank/NBFC for home loan with PMAY subsidy
offline: Visit nearest bank branch (SBI, HDFC, ICICI, etc.) or housing finance company
Bank verifies eligibility and forwards to NHB/HUDCO
Subsidy credited to loan account after approval

fees.offline: Free (bank processing fees may apply for loan)

pmay_urban.processing_time: Application: 30-60 days; Subsidy credit: 3-6 months after loan disbursement
pmay_urban.disbursement: Interest subsidy credited directly to home loan account
pmay_urban.description: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban 2.0 is a central government housing scheme providing interest subsidy on home loans for first-time homeowners in urban areas. The scheme covers four income categories: EWS (up to ₹3 lakh/year), LIG (up to ₹6 lakh), MIG-I (up to ₹12 lakh), and MIG-II (up to ₹18 lakh). The key condition is that no family member should own a pucca house anywhere in India. The interest subsidy reduces EMI burden significantly over the loan tenure.
notes: Must be first-time homeowner — no pucca house owned by any family member
Subsidy is credited to the loan account, not as cash
Woman ownership/co-ownership is mandatory for EWS/LIG categories
Carpet area limit: 30 sq.m (EWS), 60 sq.m (LIG), 160 sq.m (MIG-I), 200 sq.m (MIG-II)
Approach any scheduled bank or housing finance company for PMAY-linked loan

jagananna_vidya_deevena.name: Jagananna Vidya Deevena

benefit_breakdown.fee_reimbursement: 100% of tuition fee (as per government fee structure)
benefit_breakdown.maintenance: ₹20,000 per year for food and hostel expenses
benefit_breakdown.total_value: Varies by course (₹50,000 to ₹2,00,000+ per year)

course_eligible: PG courses (MA, MSc, MCom, MBA, MCA)

online: College principal verifies and forwards application
Fee credited directly to college account
Maintenance allowance credited to student's bank account
offline: Collect application form from college administration office
Submit to college principal for verification
College forwards to district social welfare office

jagananna_vidya_deevena.processing_time: College verification: 15 days; District approval: 30-45 days; Disbursement: Every semester
jagananna_vidya_deevena.disbursement: Fee directly to college; Maintenance to student's bank account
jagananna_vidya_deevena.deadline: Apply within 30 days of college admission each academic year
jagananna_vidya_deevena.description: Jagananna Vidya Deevena is Andhra Pradesh's flagship college education assistance scheme providing full fee reimbursement and ₹20,000 annual maintenance allowance to students from economically backward classes (EBC), backward classes (BC), scheduled castes (SC), scheduled tribes (ST), and minority communities. The scheme covers degree, engineering, medical, polytechnic, ITI, and PG courses. Students must have 75% attendance and family income below ₹8 lakh per year.
notes: Apply within 30 days of admission — late applications may be rejected
Attendance below 75% leads to discontinuation of benefits
Fee is paid directly to college, not to student
Maintenance allowance is credited semester-wise (₹10,000 per semester)
Students in private colleges are also eligible if college is recognized
Renewal required every academic year with updated marksheet

post_matric_scholarship.name: Post-Matric Scholarship (SC/ST/OBC/Minority)
post_matric_scholarship.benefit: Maintenance allowance + fee reimbursement for SC/ST/OBC/Minority students

benefit_breakdown.maintenance_allowance: Varies by course level (₹230 to ₹1,200 per month)
benefit_breakdown.course_fee: Up to ₹1.5 lakh per year for professional courses

income_limits.OBC: Annual family income below ₹1.5 lakh (non-creamy layer)

documents: Current year admission receipt / Fee receipt

online: Select 'Post Matric Scholarship' for your category
Institute forwards to district welfare officer
offline: Collect form from college scholarship cell or social welfare office
College verifies and forwards to district

post_matric_scholarship.processing_time: Institute verification: 15 days; District: 30 days; State sanction: 45-60 days
post_matric_scholarship.disbursement: Direct to student's bank account via PFMS
post_matric_scholarship.deadline: Apply between July and November each academic year
post_matric_scholarship.description: The Post-Matric Scholarship scheme is a central government initiative providing financial assistance to SC, ST, OBC, and minority students pursuing education after Class 10. It covers maintenance allowance, course fees, book allowance, and study tour expenses. SC/ST students have no income limit. OBC students must be non-creamy layer with income below ₹1.5 lakh. Minority students must have income below ₹2 lakh. Applications are processed through the National Scholarship Portal (NSP).
notes: Apply only through National Scholarship Portal — no other channel
Institute must be registered on NSP for verification
Renewal required every year with updated marksheet
Aadhaar-bank linkage mandatory for PFMS disbursement
Check application status regularly on NSP portal
If rejected, check reason and reapply with corrections

pragati_scholarship.name: Pragati Scholarship (Girl Students)
pragati_scholarship.benefit: ₹50,000 per year + full tuition fee reimbursement

benefit_breakdown.tuition_fee: Up to ₹30,000 per year (actual or ceiling, whichever is lower)

online: Register with Aadhaar-linked mobile and email
Fill personal, academic, and admission details
Amount credited to student's bank account
offline: Not available — online only through AICTE portal

pragati_scholarship.processing_time: Institute verification: 15 days; AICTE sanction: 30-45 days
pragati_scholarship.disbursement: Direct to girl student's bank account in two installments per year
pragati_scholarship.deadline: Apply within 2 months of admission each academic year
pragati_scholarship.description: Pragati Scholarship is an AICTE scheme for girl students pursuing technical education (B.Tech, B.Pharm, Diploma in Engineering, etc.) in AICTE-approved institutions. It provides up to ₹50,000 per year covering tuition fee and maintenance allowance. The scheme aims to empower girls in STEM fields. Family income must be below ₹8 lakh per year. Admission must be through central counseling (JEE, state CET, etc.).
notes: Only for technical courses in AICTE-approved institutions
Admission through centralized counseling is mandatory
Maximum 2 girl children per family can avail
Renewal requires passing previous year with minimum marks
Apply early — seats are limited and first-come-first-served
